hook: AI gave me a 47-step plan when I asked how to launch my side project. I have ADHD. I needed 3 steps.
caption:
AI gave me a 47-step plan when I asked how to launch my side project. I have ADHD. I needed 3 steps.

This is the thing nobody talks about.

AI isn't bad at advice. It's bad at knowing *who it's talking to.*

Same prompt. Two different people.

Person A is a systems thinker who wants the full roadmap, every dependency mapped, nothing left vague.

Person B needs momentum, not a masterplan. Three actions. Today. Go.

The AI? It answers the words. Not the person.

So Person B gets a beautiful, comprehensive, completely paralyzing 47-step document that lives in their notes app forever.

This is exactly what BootFile fixes.

Your BootFile tells the AI things it has no way of knowing otherwise — how you process information, what makes you freeze vs. flow, whether you want the full picture or just the next door to walk through.

It's not a prompt. It's a profile. And you carry it into every conversation.

If you've ever closed an AI response and thought "this is technically correct and completely useless to me" — that's not a you problem.

That's a calibration problem.

What's one way AI responses consistently miss how YOUR brain works? Drop it below 👇
